behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI parsing/output across subprocess + filesystem. MOCK-MISS: this is not just local logic; it depends on how `git` reports renames in a real repo. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked diff/status output can miss real rename edge cases (similarity thresholds, index/worktree state), so a fake could pass while production fails. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text "Renamed files appear in their own category" requires real rename semantics from the repository toolchain, which disqualifies lower-level in-process-only wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it with content preserved, run the CLI, and assert report shows +1 renamed and +0 added/+0 deleted for that change set. Execute via real CLI process and real `git` repo on disk.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real binary diff behavior from git subprocess and real filesystem content. REAL-INFRA: binary handling is tied to actual repo files and command output. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing diff text can hide encoding/null-byte/binary marker behaviors that trigger crashes only with real git output. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text "Diffs that include binary files" disqualifies fake-only tests because exact binary diff behavior is external-tool dependent.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, commit a binary file (e.g., random bytes), modify or add another binary file, run the CLI process, assert zero crash (exit 0) and binary file changes are included in category counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This failure is commonly caused by real git behavior on unborn HEAD (exit codes/messages), which is subprocess/OS interaction. REAL-INFRA: requires real repository state with no commits. MOCK-HIDE: mocked git responses can miss real command failure modes and stderr parsing differences. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text "repository that has no commits yet" specifically targets a real git state boundary, so in-process-only tests are insufficient for minimum real-failure coverage.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo without creating any commit, run the CLI, assert it exits successfully and prints a structurally valid empty report (all categories zero/empty).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Behavior validates end-to-end correctness against actual repository history, crossing CLI, git subprocess, parsing, and aggregation modules. REAL-INFRA: real git history traversal is required. MOCK-HIDE: canned responses may accidentally align with expected counts while hiding bugs in command selection/range handling under real history. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text "actual change history of the repository" disqualifies purely mocked module wiring as the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo with a known sequence of commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies; run the CLI over that repo; compare reported per-category counts to precomputed ground truth from the constructed history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics, which are external tool + filesystem state interactions. REAL-INFRA: staged/unstaged distinctions come from real index/worktree behavior. MOCK-HIDE: faking staged/unstaged data can miss real discrepancies in command flags, index refresh, and pathspec behavior. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text "staged or unstaged" explicitly requires real git state transitions, disqualifying lower levels for minimum real-failure detection.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent file changes in two scenarios (staged vs unstaged), run the CLI in each scenario, normalize non-deterministic fields if any (timestamps), and assert identical structured output.
